evidence_quotes.quote: Many of the video games nowadays bring fort what i consider "Planed Obsolesce" in which after a certain period, these medium of entertainment, stop working at all.

paragraphs.text: Video Games have become an entertainment medium that have amassed a big audience over the last years. It is no longer something designated to a specific audience, with few participants. It has become a medium so big that online culture has been impacted by it. Videos, Livestreams, Forums, etc...all of these forms of online communication, are used to promote and engage online and they create community's that share the same love for a specific video game or video games in general. That brings huge amounts of money to its field, and from it companies grew and have been growing. Selling Video Games has become a viable method of revenue. However, there's been a problem within the space that has become more and more apparent. Many of the video games nowadays bring fort what i consider "Planed Obsolesce" in which after a certain period, these medium of entertainment, stop working at all. Companies effectively stop supporting a game so that the product i have bought has become unusable, with nothing we can do to it. It is as if i had bought a car but after a certain amount of time, in which is decided by the company, they take the wheels and engine of it. We cannot repair it, we cannot ride it. Many companies hide behind the fact that they give a timeframe to when they will stop the support, but in the end, they still leave a product that was bought by thousands of costumers inaccessible. I write this feedback to alert the European union in hopes that this type of behavior becomes more regulated so that video game companies when publishing a game have a plan to when they eventually stop supporting a game, the product that they sold to their costumers isn't unusable, such as, letting the consumers support themselves the medium or having enough of the medium intact so that consumers can enjoy their purchase even after support has ended. I do not expect companies to provide support of video games forever, but i do expect being able to enjoy a product i bought and not live with the fear that my favorite videogame might not be playable, in the future.
